taskDescription: Drag the correct histological terms into the blanks to complete statements about nervous tissue.
textField:
Multipolar neurons have a large polygonal *soma* with abundant *Nissl* bodies in the cytoplasm, representing rough endoplasmic reticulum. Nissl bodies are present in the cell body and *dendrites* but are absent from the *axon hillock* and axon.

In the PNS, myelin is produced by *Schwann* cells, while in the CNS it is produced by *oligodendrocytes*. A single Schwann cell myelinates only one *internode*, whereas an oligodendrocyte can myelinate multiple axons simultaneously.

A peripheral nerve is surrounded by *epineurium* sheath externally; each fascicle within it is wrapped by *perineurium* sheath. Individual nerve fibres are enclosed by the *endoneurium*. The blood-nerve barrier is formed primarily by the perineurium.

In Guillain-Barre syndrome, autoimmune *demyelination* of peripheral nerves causes prolonged distal motor *latencies* on nerve conduction studies. The *astrocytes* are the most abundant glial cells in the CNS and contribute to the blood-brain barrier.

overallFeedback.feedback: Review the histology of nervous tissue.
Good effort! Review the details you missed.
